content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS with length NNN and you should change it to a palindrome using a sequence of zero or more operations. In one operation, you should choose two adjacent elements of SSS (two characters) and swap them; however, neither of these elements may be used in any other operation.

Find the minimum number of operations you have to perform in order to change the string SSS into a palindrome, or determine that it is impossible.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to make the string SSS a palindrome, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print two lines. The first of these lines should contain the string `"YES"` (without quotes). The second line should contain a single integer ― the minimum required number of operations.
